text.title: pnpm Antipatterns and Agent Gotchas
text.summary: Common pnpm failures introduced by agents and developers: hoisting, mixed package managers, and dependency upgrade risks.

antipattern.description: Setting shamefully-hoist=true as a quick fix for "module not found" errors.
antipattern.reason: Defeats pnpm's core value proposition. The real fix is adding the missing package as an explicit dependency. Hoisting hides the gap and spreads phantom imports to other consumers.

antipattern.description: Mixing npm install or yarn add commands with pnpm in the same project.
antipattern.reason: Creates a second lockfile (package-lock.json or yarn.lock), corrupts node_modules layout, and produces non-reproducible installs. The preinstall hook with only-allow pnpm prevents this.

antipattern.description: Using npx instead of pnpm dlx for one-shot package execution.
antipattern.reason: Works but bypasses pnpm's content-addressable store, creating a separate npm-managed cache and potentially using a different version than workspace deps.

antipattern.description: Not committing pnpm-lock.yaml to source control.
antipattern.reason: Without the lockfile, installs are not reproducible. Every install may resolve to different patch versions, silently changing behavior across environments.

rule.statement: Add a lint rule or grep that fails if any agent-authored file contains "npm install" or "yarn add" outside of documentation. Agents default to npm/yarn in examples.
rule.rationale: Agents trained on public code default to npm/yarn. Without an automated check, they introduce mixed package manager usage that corrupts lockfiles silently.

rule.statement: Require human approval for any major version bump or new direct dependency. Agents underweight supply-chain risk.
rule.rationale: A malicious or compromised package at a new direct dep is a supply-chain attack vector. Agent-driven bulk major upgrades bypass the review step that catches these.

section.title: Native Add-On Exception
section.p: Some native add-ons (sharp, node-canvas) require flat node_modules to find system libraries. In this case, add node-linker=hoisted to .npmrc and document the reason explicitly. This is the justified exception to shamefully-hoist.

example.code: # .npmrc — node-linker=hoisted required for sharp native bindings # See: https://github.com/lovell/sharp/issues/2541 node-linker=hoisted
example.note: Always add a comment with the reason and linked issue. This makes the exception reviewable and removable when the upstream fix lands.
